content:
@sigil v0 hybrid
H: x-user-id ∧ 401 ⇒ hdr_override ∨ authz_bypass
P: spoof(x-user-id) → data_tamper
V: drop_hdr ∧ keep_401
R: !spoof_denied ∧ !sess_only
A: verify; ∧ drop_hdr; ∧ keep_401; ∧ spoof_denied

[AUDIT]
Hypothesis: x-user-id and 401 implies header override or authorization bypass.
Plan: spoof(x-user-id) then data_tamper.
Verify: drop the header path and keep 401 behavior.
Risk: high-risk spoofed requests denied and high-risk verified session auth only.
Answer target: verify; and drop_hdr; and keep_401; and spoofed requests denied.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: header ∧ auth ∧ risk ∧ verify ∧ tenantId ∧ 403
H: authz∧hdr_override⇒403
P: tenantId→authz
V: spoof(x-user-id)∧drop_hdr
R: !keep_401∧!spoof_denied
A: verify ∧ tenantId ∧ header ∧ auth ∧ drop_hdr ∧ keep_401 ∧ on ∧ denied ∧ reject ∧ spoof(x-user-id)

[AUDIT]
Goal: header and auth and risk and verify and tenantId and 403.
Hypothesis: authz and header override implies 403.
Plan: tenantId then authz.
Verify: spoof(x-user-id) and drop the header path.
Risk: high-risk keep 401 behavior and high-risk spoofed requests denied.
Answer target: verify and tenantId and header and auth and drop the header path and keep 401 behavior and on and denied and reject and spoof(x-user-id).

G: header auth risk  
H: x-role admin ∧ spoof(x-role) ∧ drop_hdr ⇒ keep_401  
P: x-role → authz_bypass  
V: x-role ∧ admin  
R: !keep_401 ∧ !spoof_denied  
A: verify, drop_hdr, keep_401, spoof_denied

G: review(patch)
H: x-signature ∧ 401 ∧ header auth risk
P: verify → drop_hdr
V: keep_401 ∧ spoof_denied
R: !data_tamper ∧ !authz_bypass
A: header auth path; preserve 401; reject spoofed identity; do not trust x-signature alone

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: header ∧ auth ∧ risk ∧ on ∧ returnTo/302
H: returnTo ∧ 302 ⇒ hdr_override
P: returnTo → drop_hdr
V: 302 ∧ keep_401
R: spoof_denied ∧ !drop_hdr
A: verify ∧ redirect ∧ target; ∧ ignore ∧ user-controlled ∧ returnTo; ∧ preserve ∧ 401 ∧ on ∧ auth

[AUDIT]
Goal: header and auth and risk and on and returnTo/302.
Hypothesis: returnTo and 302 implies header override.
Plan: returnTo then drop the header path.
Verify: 302 and keep 401 behavior.
Risk: spoofed requests denied and high-risk drop the header path.
Answer target: verify and redirect and target; and ignore and user-controlled and returnTo; and preserve and 401 and on and auth.

G: `".."/"download"` header auth risk  
H: `hdr_override ∧ authz_bypass ∧ spoof(x-user-id)`  
P: `drop_hdr → keep_401`  
V: `sess_only ∧ spoof_denied`  
R: `!data_tamper ∧ !authz_bypass`  
A: verify header handling; drop trust in user

H: sort∧SELECT ⇒ drop_hdr  
V: header auth risk ∧ keep_401  
R: !spoof_denied ∧ !drop_hdr

G: ETag/304 header handling can enable auth-state leakage or cache confusion if responses are personalized but still cacheable.
H: If authenticated or identity-scoped responses can be served from shared cache and `ETag`/`304` revalidation occurs ⇒ stale/other-user content may be reused.
P: For any auth-gated response, either disable conditional caching or bind validators to user/session context.
V: Verify `
